ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_type" INTEGER /* Type of sensor (e.g., acceleration, temperature, vibration) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the sensor data */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was installed */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the sensor in the facility */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Last date the sensor was calibrated */,
  "calibration_due_date" TEXT /* Next date the sensor is due for calibration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the sensor (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the sensor data */,
  "frequency_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sampling frequency */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the last user who interacted with the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_sensor_type FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_type") REFERENCES sensor_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_frequency_id FOREIGN KEY ("frequency_id") REFERENCES sampling_frequencies ("frequency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that collected the data */,
  "sample_no" INTEGER /* Sample number of the data point */,
  "x_axis" REAL /* X-axis value of the data point */,
  "y_axis" REAL /* Y-axis value of the data point */,
  "z_axis" REAL /* Z-axis value of the data point */,
  "timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the data point */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the data point */,
  "quality_flag" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the data point is considered valid */,
  "anomaly_detected" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if an anomaly was detected in the data point */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last reviewed the data point */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the data point */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_sample_no FOREIGN KEY ("sample_no") REFERENCES sampling_frequencies ("frequency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor type */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value the sensor type can measure */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value the sensor type can measure */,
  "default_resolution" TEXT /* Default resolution for the sensor type */,
  "default_frequency_id" INTEGER /* ID of the default sampling frequency for the sensor type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sampling_frequencies" (
  "frequency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sampling frequency */,
  "frequency_name" TEXT /* Name of the sampling frequency */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sampling frequency */,
  "frequency_value" REAL /* Numerical value of the sampling frequency */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the sampling frequency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frequency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_health" (
  "health_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health record */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the health record */,
  "health_score" REAL /* Numerical score indicating the health of the sensor */,
  "maintenance_due_date" TEXT /* Next date the sensor is due for maintenance */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Last date the sensor was maintained */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the health record */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the health record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("health_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_health_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_health_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, operator) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last date the user logged into the system */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Last date the user was active in the system */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., operations, maintenance, quality control) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
